project_type: Android app with Kotlin + Jetpack Compose, backed by Laravel API
test_framework: JUnit 4 + Espresso + Compose UI test (minimal tests currently)
verification_instructions: Run ./gradlew assembleDebug in the MysticNusa directory to verify compilation succeeds
setup_instructions: Java 17 is required. Run ./gradlew assembleDebug from MysticNusa/
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access. Java 17+ available via mise.
contribution_requirements: Follow existing Kotlin code patterns, use Jetpack Compose for UI
key_patterns: MVVM architecture: NgepetViewModel manages state via StateFlow<NgepetUiState>, NgepetGameScreen.kt renders phases. API calls go through GamesRepository -> ApiService (Retrofit). Models in NgepetModels.kt. Sound effects referenced in web frontend: intruderSound (wrong guess), intruderwinSound (correct guess/win), intruderloseSound (game over/lose). Backend POST /ngepet/match/{id}/hidden-guess expects: hidden_item_id, match_intruder_id, item_name. Returns: {status:'guess_submitted', is_correct:bool, is_end:bool, answer_item:string|null} or {error:string}.
directory_structure: MysticNusa/ = Android project root. app/src/main/java/com/mysticnusa/app/ has data/ (models, remote, repository), ui/ (screens, viewmodels, components, theme). app/src/main/res/ has drawable, values, xml (no raw folder yet for sounds). resources/views/ = web frontend (Blade templates). Controllers/ = Laravel backend.
